description_long: This is a vintage corded telephone featuring a rectangular base with a brown and beige color scheme. The base houses a prominent rotary dial at the center, surrounded by a speaker grille and several control buttons or indicators along the top edge. A dark brown handset is connected to the base by a long, coiled black cord, which is visibly flexible and extends outward. The handset is ergonomically shaped, with a speaker and microphone at either end, and rests on a molded cradle integrated into the base. The overall design is robust, with the base likely made of hard plastic and some internal metal components for durability. This type of phone is typically found in private homes or offices from the mid-to-late 20th century.
description: A vintage brown and beige corded telephone with a rotary dial, coiled cord, and a matching handset resting on a molded cradle. The design is robust and classic, suitable for home or office use.
description_view_0: Front view showing the rotary dial, speaker grille, and the handset resting on the cradle with the coiled cord extending outward.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the sloped profile of the base, the rotary dial, and the full length of the handset and coiled cord.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the base, the cord attachment, and the handset lying away from the cradle.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the rotary dial, the side of the handset, and the coiled cord looping around the base.

license_info.creator_display_name: Museum of Engineering and Technology, Krakow
